document_title: FORM FDA 3632 - Guide for Preparing Product Reports for Lasers
regulatory_authority: FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health (CDRH)

executive_summary.purpose: Guide for manufacturers to prepare Product Reports for lasers and products containing lasers
executive_summary.legal_basis: 21 CFR Parts 1002 and 1003 - Reporting and Notification requirements
executive_summary.key_message: CDRH does NOT approve reports or products - manufacturers are responsible for certification based on testing programs in accordance with good manufacturing practices

product_report.purpose: Initial report describing product, compliance details, and quality control program
product_report.scope: Can be used for families of products as well as individual products
product_report.timing: Prior to introduction of products into commerce (including imports)

supplemental_report.purpose: Supplementary information to previously submitted Product Report
triggers: New model in previously reported model family
Changes affecting actual or potential emission
Changes decreasing degree of compliance with performance standard
Changes resulting in decreased probability of detecting noncompliance
supplemental_report.requirement: Must clearly reference CDRH accession number of original Product Report

annual_report.purpose: Summarizes required records; identifies new models not requiring Supplemental Reports

part_1.title: Manufacturer and Report Identification
requirements: Manufacturing firm name and address
Corresponding official with signature
Prime contact or responsible person
Importing agent information (if applicable, per 21 CFR 1005.25)
Report type (Product Report or Supplement to specific accession number)

part_2.title: Product and Model Identification
requirements: All names, brand names, model numbers, and model family designations
Identification of other companies selling under different brand names
Documentation of modifications to certified products from other manufacturers
Documentation of incorporated laser products (certified or non-certified)
Removable laser system identification
Recommended laser specifications

part_4.title: Compliance with Informational Requirements
requirements: User and servicing information (operator and service manuals)
Catalogs, specification sheets, descriptive brochures for Class IIa, II, III, IV
Warning logotype reproduction in all promotional materials

part_5.title: Description of the Product
requirements: Product description and function
External and internal laser radiation fields and paths
Operational procedures with accessible radiation identification
Maintenance procedures with accessible radiation identification
Service procedures with accessible radiation identification

part_6.title: Levels of Accessible Laser Radiation and Classification
requirements: Specifications of all accessible laser radiation fields during operation
Product classification (Class I, IIa, II, IIIa, IIIb, or IV)
Specifications for maintenance and service access
Collateral radiation description
Measurement parameters per 1040.10(e)

subsections.applies_to: Class IIIb or IV laser systems
Class IIIb or IV laser systems
Class II, IIIa, IIIb, IV laser systems
Class II, IIIa, IIIb, IV laser systems
Class II, IIIa, IIIb, IV laser systems
Class II, IIIa, IIIb, IV laser products
Class IV laser systems after Aug 20, 1986

part_8.title: Quality Control Tests and Testing Procedures
requirements: Specification controls for critical components
Manufacturing and assembly control procedures
Inspection and test control procedures
Assembly and test traveler forms
Inspection and test reports and checklists
part_8.legal_note: Section 1010.2(c) requires certification based on testing in accordance with standard or program in accordance with good manufacturing practices

part_9.title: Life and Endurance Testing
requirements: Dimensional stability and rigidity of mechanical parts
Design and ratings of electrical/electronic components
Environmental stability of components (filters, coatings, adhesives)
Design and testing of features for compliance
Other factors affecting radiation safety

part_10.title: Instrumentation and Calibration
requirements: List of instruments used for compliance determination
Description of measurement system
Measurement error analysis and uncertainty statement
Instrument calibration schedules and procedures
Traceability to NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology)

submission_information.mailing_address: Center for Devices and Radiological Health, Document Mail Center - WO66-G609, Attn: Electronic Product Reports, 10903 New Hampshire Avenue, Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002
submission_information.accession_number: Unique identifier assigned upon receipt for future reference

enforcement_notes: CDRH may reject incomplete or inadequate reports
Rejected reports do not receive accession numbers
CDRH may disapprove firm's quality control and testing program
CDRH may determine product contains radiation defect or fails to comply
CDRH may require cessation of introduction into commerce
